description: Supabase database patterns, migrations, and safety rules for Sovereign-generated apps.

rls.rule: RLS must be enabled on every table that stores user data — no exceptions
rls.command: ALTER TABLE <table> ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

policy_examples.insert_anon: CREATE POLICY anon_insert ON waitlist FOR INSERT TO anon WITH CHECK (true);
policy_examples.select_own_rows: CREATE POLICY select_own ON profiles FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
policy_examples.update_own_rows: CREATE POLICY update_own ON profiles FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = user_id);
policy_examples.service_role_only: No policies needed — service role key bypasses RLS automatically

rls.verification: SELECT tablename, rowsecurity FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = 'public';
rls.sovereign_lesson: CVE-2025-48757 — 170+ apps on a competing platform exposed because RLS was missing. This is why it is the first rule.

migrations.idempotency: Always use IF NOT EXISTS and IF EXISTS to make migrations safe to re-run

examples.add_column: ALTER TABLE builds ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NULL;
examples.create_table: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS magic_links (...);
examples.add_index: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS magic_links_token_idx ON magic_links(token);

migrations.verification: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = '<table>' AND column_name = '<column>';
migrations.sovereign_rule: Migration files are NEVER auto-run in Supabase — they must be manually executed in the SQL Editor and verified. Never assume a migration ran. Verify.

soft_deletes.rule: Never hard-delete user data — add deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NULL to every user-data table
soft_deletes.query_pattern: Always add WHERE deleted_at IS NULL to queries that should exclude deleted rows
soft_deletes.migration: ALTER TABLE <table> ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NULL;
soft_deletes.rationale: Regulatory compliance, audit requirements, accidental deletion recovery, user trust

indexing.rule: Add indexes to all frequently queried columns — especially foreign keys and filter columns
common_indexes: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_builds_user_id ON builds(user_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_builds_status ON builds(status) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_magic_links_token ON magic_links(token);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_magic_links_email ON magic_links(email);
indexing.partial_indexes: Use WHERE clause on indexes for queries that always filter on a condition (e.g. WHERE deleted_at IS NULL)

connection_pooling.supabase_default: Supabase uses PgBouncer in transaction mode by default — safe for serverless
connection_pooling.rule: Use the pooler connection string (port 6543) for serverless functions, not the direct connection (port 5432)
connection_pooling.serverless_warning: Direct connections in serverless will exhaust Postgres connection limits — always use the pooler

standard_columns: id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
schema_patterns.user_scoping: Add user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE to every user-owned table
schema_patterns.build_scoping: Add build_id UUID NOT NULL to every table in generated apps that belongs to a build

verification_queries.check_rls: SELECT tablename, rowsecurity FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname = 'public';
verification_queries.check_column_exists: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = '<t>' AND column_name = '<c>';
verification_queries.check_table_exists: SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = '<t>';
verification_queries.check_policies: SELECT tablename, policyname, cmd FROM pg_policies WHERE schemaname = 'public';
verification_queries.verify_all_schema: Run api/migrations/verify-schema.sql for a full check of all required columns
